Petition For Contribution From Co-Trustee Leonard Horowitz For Purported Breaches Of Trust Committed By Co-Trustee Sally Gati
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Continued To Apr-20-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lenz appeared via zoom for and with Sally Gati (petitioner).
Zach Haydt appeared via zoom for and with Deborah Kilinger (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding trial setting, that parties are working on discovery and motions up to the trial date.

Status on mediation is off calendar.

The matter is set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on April 20, 2026 at 9:30 a.m.,

Judge: Josh Fredricks. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Espin. Not reported.

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Continued To Apr-20-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Off Calendar

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Continued To Oct-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Continued To Oct-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Lenz for Sally Gati, Co-Trustee (via Zoom).
Zach Haydt for and with Deborah Klinger, Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Sally Gati was present in the courtroom.

Mr. Haydt explained that the prior attorney from his firm had left the case, and due to the transition and a brief health issue, the parties had not yet completed mediation. Both counsel agreed that more time was needed to adequately prepare and conduct meaningful settlement discussions. Counsel agreed that mediation could be completed within 60 days and proposed returning to court approximately three weeks thereafter.

The Court ordered the parties to complete mediation by September 19, 2025, and set a further status hearing for October 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court executed a Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation and directed the parties to obtain a copy of the order from the court's online docket.

The underlying matters were also continued to October 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Contribution From Co-Trustee Leonard Horowitz For Purported Breaches Of Trust Committed By Co-Trustee Sally Gati
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Continued To Oct-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Referral To Pro Bono Mediation
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Contribution From Co-Trustee Leonard Horowitz For Purported Breaches Of Trust Committed By Co-Trustee Sally Gati
Off Calendar May-12-2025 Continued To Jul-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sean Buck for and with Deborah Klinger, Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Christopher Lenz (via Zoom) for and with Sally Gati (in person), Co-Trustee.

The Court held a discussion regarding the timeframe for mediation. Following this discussion, the Court set a six-week period for mediation and issued a Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation. The Court directed the parties to complete mediation no later than June 30, 2025, and instructed them to notify the assigned Probate Examiner of the mediation status by July 7, 2025. A Status Hearing on Mediation was scheduled for July 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court continued the two petitions to the same date for hearing and removed the Status on Referral to Pro Bono Mediation from the calendar. Counsel were instructed to obtain a copy of the Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation from the Court's website.

Further discussion was held regarding the request for a Breslin Order. Mr. Buck indicated that Co-Trustee Leonard Horowitz might not participate in the mediation. After further discussion, the Court agreed to issue a Breslin Order following the hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Continued To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sean Buck for Deborah Klinger, Petitioner/Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Christopher Lenz for Sally Gati, Respondent/Co-Trustee (via Zoom).
Deborah Klinger is present on Zoom.
Sally Gati is present in court.

Counsel reported progress on discovery, such as the completion of the deposition of Sally Gati and document production but noted that additional discovery may still be pursued.

Both sides expressed openness to mediation or ADR but have yet to conduct meaningful discussions following the deposition. Mr. Lenz indicated that they are considering filing a motion for judgment on the pleadings and will meet and confer with petitioner's counsel before proceeding.

Discussion was held regarding the possibility of engaging in pro bono mediation and counsel were informed they may file an ex parte application for referral if they agree to proceed with mediation before the next hearing.

The court continued the petition March 26, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and also set a status hearing on referral to pro bono mediation for the same day.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts
Off Calendar Oct-14-2024 Continued To Dec-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sean Buck appeared by zoom for Deborah Klinger (petitioner).
Christopher Lenz appeared by zoom for and with Sally Gati (co-trustee) in person.

Mr. Buck stated that they have been doing discovery, that there has been a delay due to some medical issues and asked for a continuance until December with the expectation that discovery will be done by then. Mr. Lenz stated that there is no notice of today's hearing, asked that notice of all hearings be given as there are other parties who are not represented by him or by Mr. Buck, and asked that those parties be given notice. Mr. Buck stated that they have broached the option of ADR once discovery is completed. The Petition for Order Determining Questions of Interpretation of Trust, etc. is continued to December 18, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petitioner is to give notice of the hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Saenz. Not reported.

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts
Off Calendar Jul-08-2024 Continued To Oct-14-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Philip Ye appeared by zoom for Deborah Klinger.
Christopher Lenz appeared by zoom for and with Sally Gati in person.

Counsel requested a 90-day continuance to try and resolve the issue of obtaining responses to discovery that has already been submitted. The court informed the parties that informal discovery with a Judge is available to them, and that pro bono mediation is also available to them if they are interested. The Petition for Order Determining Questions of Interpretation of Trust and Instructing Trustee to Follow Terms of Trust, etc. is continued to October 14, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Continued To Jul-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Christopher Lenz appeared via zoom for and with Sally Gati (Respondent).
Sean Buck appeared via zoom for and with Deborah Klinger (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding discovery, that parties need more time to exchange discovery, and that both parties need to meet and confer about a possibility for future mediation. The Court orders respondent/co-trustee not use trust assets to pay for defense. Matter is continued to July 08, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Aquino. Not reported.

Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Savannah Andre, Esq., for Deborah Klinger, Petitioner.
Chris Lenz, Esq., for Sally Gati, in court, interested party.

At the outset, the court confirms Mr. Lenz' intent to file an objection to the instant petition. After additional discussion, the court advises that it intends to continue the matter to Apr-24-2024 and offers the following briefing schedule:

1) Written objection to the instant petition is to be filed by Mar-27-2024.

2) Written response is to be filed by Apr-10-2024.

Counsel agrees to the schedule as stated above and the matter is continued to Apr-24-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
